commentary: Back of a length ball on off stump, he stands tall and pushes to extra cover
Slower length ball on middle and leg, he works this with soft hands to deep midwicket. After an 18-run opening over, Payne has returned with a two run and two wicket over.
Slower back of a length ball on off stump, he pulls to deep midwicket for a run
On-pace length ball on off stump, he eases this to long-on
On the fuller side of length, David brings out his big bat swing but mistimes it just over the bowler's head on the bounce
Slower bouncer banged in short around off stump shoulder-high. He taps this to backward point and sets off only to be sent back by David
Another offcutter, was a back of a length ball outside off, he mistimes this to sweeper cover

Slower bouncer but that is too high even for the lanky David, going over his head. Called a wide, but Kishan reviews. SRH only have a review left, so he might think this is close. He is wrong. Comfortably and wide and SRH have now lost both reviews, both on wides .
Slower bouncer angling across David outside off. He flat-bats this to mid-off, loses his shape completely in doing that
Another day, another fifty for Kohli... no 64 in the IPL for him. He starts IPL 2026 the way he left the previous season Gets to his fifty by pulling Payne to deep midwicket

A tennis forehand and David gets four to his name. Slower bouncer well outside off, he finally gets a clean chunk of it and flat-bats it to the right of cover. Reddy gets across and comically goes outside the boundary ropes, trying to stop that.
Banged in short outside off, he pulls to deep midwicket
Back of a length ball angling across Kohli, he gets this to deep cover
On-pace 127kph ball right in his wheelhouse. Kohli gets under it and with a clean swing of the blade smashes him over long-on
Banged in short but the line is down leg, he helps this with ease over the keeper's head for four more
Oh, my word! This is a shot and a half.. Kohli at his finest. Length ball outside off, he jumps down the track and goes aerially over covers with minimum of fuss

That's that! Defending champions start IPL 2026 with a statement win. At the halfway mark, the question was is 200 enough? The way RCB went about the chase, even 250 wasn't going to be enough tonight. They have galloped past the 202 target in 15.4 overs and with some style. Kohli ends the game with another boundary. Slower short ball outside off, he ramps this over short third and ends with an unbeaten 69 off 38. RCB win by six wickets with 26 balls to spare.
Short and wide again, this time he just manages to toe end to cover. Muzarabani lucky that one didn't go in the gap as well
Tyagi nearly has a second wicket there as Tilak miscues this pull. Slower bouncer angled across him and he gets this off high on the bat. goes on the bounce to deep midwicket
Slower ball, full outside off, Pandya tries to swing it away leg side but gets an inside edge onto his pads that deflects towards cover
Good stuff from Kartik as he nails the wide yorker. Tilak tries to squeeze it away but can't reach it

Did that pitch outside leg? Looked pretty plumb otherwise! 145kph full delivery around leg and Tilak swings across the line and misses. The ball crashes into his pad. Tyagi convinces Rinku to review. Yes, that did pitch outside leg and KKR lose a review
Bouncer wide of off, pace off, Pandya toe-ends it down to long-on

That was a good over till now, but Tilak has the final word . He goes well across to this full toss outside off and scoops it over short fine. That was fired at 143/6kph, but Tilak played that with ease
Short around leg and angled across him. Pandya looks for the cut but can't make contact
Good bouncer, cramping Pandya for room. He looks to pull but gloves it into the square leg region
125kph short ball sits up nicely, and there's width on offer as well. Tilak stands tall and slashes it over backward point
Full toss on the stumps, Tilak digs it out towards long-off
Short ball angled across, Pandya this time waits on it and dabs it in the gap between deep backward point and deep third. Gets a single

That's just unlucky for the bowler! Nails the yorker as he follows Tilak down the leg side. Tilak jams his bat down and the ball ends up going through his feet and past the keeper. Umpire confers with Tilak and gives it as runs, even though it looked like it went off his boots!
On a length outside off, Pandya drills it down to long-off
On a length around off, clipped out through midwicket
Full outside off, Pandya tries to swing it away to deep midwicket. His right hand came off the handle
Tilak picks out long-on! Slowed down to 83.1kph, it's an offbreak outside off. Tilak looks to go downtown but picks out Manish Pandey, who is a safe pair of hands
On a length outside off, punched down to long-off
Finds the gap! Hardik Pandya levels the scores as he takes on this half-tracker. Pulls it away wide of long-off, an d MI are one run away

And Mumbai Indians win their first season-opener since 2012! And they do it by pulling off their biggest chase! This is full on middle and Naman Dhir just makes room and drills it down the ground. Mumbai Indians begin with a comprehensive victory!
Back of length on off, run down to deep third
Quick single to midwicket. Length ball on middle and leg, Jaiswal walks down and flicks
Short ball is high, called one for the over. Was dug in at 132ks, went around head-height just outside off. Parag shaped to upper-cut before leaving
Pitched up around off, punched to mid-off

Waits for the short one and slaps it over extra cover. It's very short, wide too. May have turned away, Parag went tennis-style, very wristily flat-batted it. Didn't go through with the follow-through fully but timed it perfectly
Catch check. Low one to Kartik at backward point, replay shows he took it on the bounce. Was a fullish ball outside off that Parag tried to punch to cover
Looks to slug it from outside off over cow corner. Gets it off the bottom-half. Along the ground to wide long-on
A bit outside off and Parag very wristy again. This time with a cut to long-off
Not sure how that missed. Stock ball ripping into a length and then going away to beat Jaiswal's straight swing
Dropped by Henry at deep-backward square. Ran in, slid with the knee going in front. Jaiswal looking to sweep it off a length, got a top edge
Goes for the reverse, the shortish ball goes off the glove to the keeper's right
Gets the reverse this time! Flat over deep point it sails. Shifted the stance but not the grip, Parag's hands were swift as he swiped low at the fullish ball at the stumps
Shot of the day! Utter disrespect charging at a pacer, smashing the scramble-seam ball from middle from well down leg. Sails over long-off
Looks to drive it straight off the front foot, gets an inside edge to wide long-on
Pulls the short ball to deep midwicket's right. Stopped with a tumble
Looks like Steve Smith as he slants himself, closes his stance and punches the full ball to long-on
Short ball just outside off is pulled into the ground. Goes to deep midwicket

That looked wide on first view. Not given, RR review. 133ks bouncer on fifth stump. Parag was on the hop as he tried to upper-cut it fine. Ball went over the bat but that was after it passed him. Head height is 1.83 metres and the ball is 1.89 metres. Decision reversed
Royals win with nearly eight overs to spare. They get there with a gentle tuck off the pads to midwicket. Sedate celebrations
played back to the bowler
Jansen now! Stands his ground as the short ball comes at him and slams it over the wide long-on boundary, you got to get it much higher than that to trouble Marco
too wide outside off, Jansen lets it go
in the blockhole from around the wicket, Connolly digs it out to cover
in the blockhole again from around the wicket, angling the ball into the left-hander, Connolley tries to whip through the leg side but misses and gets hit on the pad

short and slow outside off, Jansen had moved outside leg and was too far away from the ball in the end to play a shot, the umpire has called it wide! Gill reviews immediately. That's not a wide and the umpire has to reverse his decision
chipped in the air towards long off, it was the slower one and Connolly tried to hit it too hard, ended up mis-timing it
short of a length and wide outside off, Jansen reaches for it and tries to cut but gets beaten
tries to bowl the wide yorker but errs on the too wide side of the line

spooned to cover! First IPL wicket for Ashok Sharma and it's a big one at a crucial stage of the game. He's delivered a back of the hand slower ball at 109 kph, Jansen was into the shot too early and hit it straight to Shubman in the circle, he had to move a bit to his right to take the catch
fuller length and straighter, Bartlett nudges gently off the pads towards midwicket and they are racing back for the second
full and straight again, angling into the right-hander, played from the crease through square leg ... Ashok Sharma has bowled a superb third over

is that the game?! Bartlett goes big, very big, smashing Prasidh's pace-on short ball over the deep midwicket boundary ... simply cleared his front foot outside leg, opened up those broad shoulders and went boom! They can't find the ball ...
pushed into the covers where Rabada fumbles and allows the single
the slower short ball, Connolly waits on it and then swivels and pulls, in the air but just wide of the fielder at short fine and down to the boundary, the 22-year old is seeing this home for PBKS!
low full toss, nearly the yorker, Connolly stays steady and digs it out on the off side for a single
short ball, Bartlett pulls, to the fielder at deep midwicket on the first bounce
in the blockhole, Connolly digs it out to long on to keep strike with PBKS two runs away from victory

Cooper Connolly is a star on IPL debut, seeing PBKS home in a tricky chase, when his team-mates were losing their heads all around him! He scores the winning runs by hanging back in his crease and cutting a flat and quick ball from Sundar to the cover boundary, those fast hands of his have the final say in this topsy-turvy contest.
Back of length on middle, flicked to deep-backward square
Beaten. Went to ramp it over the keeper. Limboed to the leg side and got the bat up, the bouncer over him beat him for pace
Thats audacious, making room to Nortje before he delivers. Back of length ball over middle is cut hard to deep cover point
French cut goes to the fine-leg boundary. Back of length just outside off, seam-up and it decks back in, Stubbs is on the back foot looking to square-drive it, gets an under-edge
Back of length on off, flicked to midwicket
Back of length outside off, guided off the back foot to deep third
Length at the stumps, punched to long-on

Might be out, no. Too far back for mid-off . Badoni ran to his right but the and the ball fell well forward. Was a back of length ball just outside off that Rizvi looked to pull across. Got opened up fully
Pace off, short of length, helped to fine leg
Bouncer has been helped fine with a pull, close to deep fine leg - who is fine - but beats him. Rizvi's head was going down and he crouched a touch as he played the shot.
More ready for this. Slower one halfway down the pitch. At the stumps. Pulled flat over deep square leg. That's the game all but done. Hundred partnership up
Hard length on off, defended to the bowler's left
Length on off, punched off the back foot to cover
Hostile pace meets incredible strokeplay! No using the pace business. Stubbs stands tall and flat bats a 144ks back of length ball from just outside off. Clears long-on for an 81-metre six
Slower full ball on middle is defended softly to mid-on
Makes room, Nortje looks to follow him with a bumper but it's too short and beats the keeper too. Rizvi looked to ramp it over the keeper, needed no contact in the end
That deserved more than one. Crunched the cover drive with one foot in front of leg stump and the other well down leg. Full ball hit from around middle
Back of length just outside off, punched to cover
Final ball as it turns out. Long-hop just outside off is hauled over deep midwicket with a pull. Rizvi smiles and punches Stubbs' arms as they hug
Back of length outside off, chopped to backward point
Full outside off, pace off, and it's tucked through square leg
Reddy goes short outside off at 122kph. Ramandeep looks to flat-bat it down the ground but it hits the bat high and lobs up towards mid-off, but doesn't carry. Lucky there, the batter

Edged to short third! Is that the game for KKR? Slower ball does the trick again. Full outside off and Rinku flashes at it. Goes off the toe end of the bat straight to Unadkat at short third and he takes a neat catch falling to his right
First ball six! Pace-on short ball sits up nicely and Narine just swivels and pulls it off his hips over square leg

Palmed over the rope! Harsh Dubey can't hold on at long-on! Slower ball, full on off. Narine takes it on and goes down the ground. Dubey leaps up, a few steps inside the rope, while running to his right, but can't quite get his hands behind the ball
Starts with a 137kph short ball and Ramandeep is hurried as he tries to pull. Goes off the top edge and lands short of deep midwicket running in

Flung full and wide at 105kph, going across Narine, who leaves it expecting the wide to be given. It is not, and KKR review. Looked fine to me on first viewing. Let's see what the third umpire has to say. And KKR are justified as the review shows it was just on the wide line

This time it's taken at long-on. Aniket Verma the fielder . Malinga went for the blockhole with pace off. Narine can't quite get under it as he tries to loft it down the ground. Aniket gets behind the line and makes a timely jump to grab it above head height, while making sure the momentum doesn't take him over the rope
Tyagi starts off with a boundary! 136kph, but drifting down leg. He just has to get some bat on it and it runs away fine on the leg side

Ouch, I think that pinged his helmet. He tries to duck under a 137kph short ball but can't evade it in time . The ball went towards long leg, allowing them the run. And now the physio comes on for an examination
Another one goes. Another catch for Unadkat . Pace-offf length ball at 108kph, Ramandeep waits on it and gets low to slash it away, but hits it straight to backward point
Lovely slower ball, full outside off, nearly takes the outside edge as Tyagi prods at it
Slower bouncer outside off, Tyagi backs away and waits on it before swatting it down to long-on
130kph length ball goes past the outside edge as Arora makes a tame push at it. Very tail-ender-ish effort from Vaibhav
Full outside off, Vaibhav manages to push it into the cover region

Gone! Umpire reckons Tyagi has nicked behind, but he's reviewed it . Full outside off, Tyagi was looking to work it leg side. There was a noise as the ball went past the inside edge. Kishan was convinced, so was Unadkat and so was the umpire. And UltraEdge confirms it. KKR go nine down

Gone, first ball and SRH are on the board! Quick yorker outside off, 130kph. Varun got the bat down but ends up inside-edging it onto the stumps. KKR are knocked over for 161 and SRH register a big, big victory
lobbed up full outside off. Driven down the ground to long off
googly floated up on a fullish length outside off. Wadhera has a wild mow at it and misses it
slot ball and punished! On middle and leg, and Shreyas clears his front leg to batter it to deep mid wicket's left!

full and wide outside off. Shreyas tries to reach out for it and fails to do so. He reckons it is wide enough to be an extra, and he reviews. Could be touch-and-go. Shreyas did not move across his stumps as such, meaning the tramlines would not have shifted a lot. Replays, though, suggest Shreyas did take a step across. The ball is well inside the revised guideline and the original call stays!

did that carry?! Another wide yorker. Shreyas jams down on it and squeezes it out towards short third. Not sure if that carried, or if it was a bump ball. Not a bump ball, and it may just have carried to Henry!

finds the fence this time, and Shreyas brings up another half-century as PBKS skipper! Full and wide again. Shreyas throws the plumbing at it and slices it over short third. A 26-ball fifty for the captain!

how has that missed?! Kamboj spears in a leg-stump yorker and it nearly catches Shreyas off-guard. The batter had moved across and then missed his flick. An extra coat of paint and leg stump may have been pegged back. But all of that might have been immaterial because Kamboj has overstepped...

straight to the sweeper, and Kamboj has another! Is it too little, too late, though? Full and wide outside off. Shreyas reaches out and gets a good chunk of it too. Not a good enough chunk to clear the fence, however, and Chahar accepts the offering with glee at sweeper cover!

oh, hello! Oh, hello! This game may not be done just yet! Hung up full and wide. Wadhera skips down and throws his hands at it. Cannot control his waft, though, and he slices it straight to Noor at extra cover. Chepauk erupts, and CSK may just have a sniff here!
first-ball boundary for Stoinis! Full and hidden a mile outside off. Stoinis chases after it and gets a thick enough outside edge to beat short third to his right!
full toss outside off. Stoinis taken aback a touch and he scuffs it back to the bowler, who leaps to his left and parries it to long off
in the air but safe! Slot ball laced with width. Shashank takes a step across and chips it over extra cover. Back for a brace
more runs over short third! Full outside off. Shashank swishes at it with pure abandon and musters a thick outside edge that clears the fielder in the ring!
low full toss outside off. Shashank drives it to sweeper cover and keeps strike
that is a stunning shot! Low full toss wide outside off. Shashank opens his bat face and scorches it through backward point!
full ball on the pads. Shashank works it into the deep square leg region and canters back for two

short and sliding a shade down leg. Shashank gets inside the line and misses his swipe. Called a wide and CSK, after a bit of thought, have reviewed. They might have a case here. Third umpire says the batter was in his normal stance. No bat involved either, meaning the wide call remains!

that will do it! A thundering blow to wrap up a commanding run-chase! Kamboj misses the yorker on the shorter side. On off and Stoinis blasts it over the bowler's head. Muted celebrations from those in the PBKS camp. This was another stellar run-chase and the best part about it, from a PBKS perspective, was that it never really seemed in doubt!
Miller happy to play the Jack Leach role. Full just outside off, defended to deep point

Short third saves three, Shardul gets low to the left. Stops it with one hand. 120ks short ball lands outside off and finishes over off. Rizvi keeps his wrists close while guiding it with an open face
No see-off business there. Miller leans across and crunches a cover drive to a decent fullish length ball. Landed just past the ring and blazed away

Slower one just outside off. Tennis-ball bounce. Let go. Ump gives it wide. Bumrah wants it reviewed. MI take it. Third umpire doesn't even bring out the measurements, sees Miller upright and the ball well above him. Calls it
Fullish length ball at the stumps, 118ks, flicked to midwicket
118ks length ball on off, defended to the bowler's right
Yorker on off, clears the front leg and swings with one hand. Goes off the toe through midwicket

Rizvi show ends and he falls looking for a six . Gets a handshake from Miller and a couple of MI players too. Was a 133ks slot ball canting into middle and leg. Rizvi bent his back leg just a bit too much and drove hard. Got it a too low, he knew he'd mistimed it right away. Long-off takes it. Been a hell of a knock though
Yorker outside off, knocks it to the off side
Pitched up and slightly down leg, beats the flick and goes off the pads to the keeper's left
Overpitched and a touch wide, bottom hand takes over as Miller drives to cover
Goes for the leg-stump yorker. Undercooks it a touch but it beats the flick and hits the pad
Low full toss on off, punched to long-on
Low full toss has been clobbered across the line, Dhir dives to the left from long-on, in vain. Miller whipping it wristily
Low full toss on off has been driven to long-off
Another yorker attempt. Shin-high full toss, punched to long-off
Yorker just outside off, jammed to the right of long-off
Full on leg, stays leg side of the ball and flicks to deep midwicket
Final ball in fact. Low full toss on leg stump is flicked fine. Races for four
clean as a whistle! Flighted up into the slot on middle and off. Rabada channels his inner Brian Lara and launches it over the bowler's head!
oh, this is beautiful batting! Very full outside off. Rashid steps down, opens his bat face and steers it between backward point and short third!
leg-cutter on a length on off. Rashid dabs it towards backward point

Kagiso Rabada, you absolute champion! This is hitting of the highest order, and hitting when the pressure is on. Slower ball on a length outside off. Rabada works out the angle from around the wicket and smokes it into the sightscreen!
low full toss wide outside off. Rabada throws his hands at it and slices it towards deep point
into the blockhole on the pads. Rashid tucks it off his toes towards deep backward square leg. Rashid takes on Jadeja's arm again and he makes it!
another top yorker on middle and off. Rashid gives himself a bit of room and clinks it out into the off side
length outside off. Rashid tries to smash it into next week and chunks it off the inside edge into his pads
sharp bumper, tucking up the batter on leg. Rabada has to work against the angle from around the wicket too and gets tagged on the body
dug in short on leg. Rabada cramped for room on the pull again and tugs it towards deep mid wicket

big, big lbw shout, and Archer wants the review. And he gets the review too! Zipped in full on middle stump. Rashid sits deep in his crease and seems to get a scratch on it before getting pinged on the back leg. Rashid thinks there is an inside edge, and the umpire's call is vindicated. UltraEdge confirms bat is involved, and Rashid stays!
into the blockhole on middle and leg. Rabada jams it back to the bowler, who gets his hands to it to his right. Vital dot ball

high full toss outside off. Rabada backs away and throws the kitchen sink at it. Misses it completely and the keeper fumbles too, allowing a bye. But hang on, there could be more drama in this still. Rabada wants to review, arguing this is above the waist. Not sure he has a case here, though. Seems to be below the waist in real-time, and Ball Tracking confirms those suspicions. 21cm below the waist, and that will be just a bye!
Deshpande starts with a wayward delivery. A full toss a mile outside off and once Rabada swings like a rusty gate and misses, the umpire calls it a wide
excellent yorker on middle and off. Rashid opens his bat face and digs it out towards deep third. Just a single, and this is getting tougher for GT now!
this is superb bowling. Another yorker outside off and Rabada can only clonk it back to the bowler, who gets his right hand to it but cannot quite prevent the single
oh my! Deshpande sticks to his guns, and Rashid cannot get his scoop away. Tries to shuffle across to this very full ball outside off and gets nutmegged for his troubles!

sliced out to the fence, and Archer snares the chance! Deshpande is having the night of his life under the Ahmedabad lights! On a length and hung up wide outside off. Rashid throws the plumbing at it but he slices it way too much. Off the outer half and Archer, prowling on the deep point fence, rattles to his right to gobble it up. Parag is pumped. Deshpande is pumped. GT on the ropes one final time perhaps!

and Deshpande has won a thriller for the Royals! Deshpande thumps his chest in pure victory elation and then does the gesture showing everyone around the country and the world that this is his stage. What a game, and what a finishing act by Deshpande! Full toss speared into the stumps. There to be hit but with the pressure on his shoulders, Ashok shanks it off the inside edge into his body. The ball rolls away harmlessly behind square and now, cue the wild celebrations. RR have pulled this one out of the fire, and Deshpande has been front and centre of GT's hopes being extinguished!
Now the yorker flies, long-off does well to cut it off. Stopped in front of the sighscreen. Ball was at the stumps and Samad drove it flat over the bowler
Slower bouncer beats him. Angling away just outside off, Pant went for a swat-cut
Slower length ball outside off is carved to deep-backward point. That's Malinga done for the day
Crowd catch back to the bowler. Pant danced down and drove the full ball back
Lot of bottom hand in that slap to cover. Full follow through, onto the back shoulder. Ball was a touch short outside off
Walks towards the bowler, gets a back of length ball darted at the stumps. Whipped to deep square leg

Taken at long-off, SRH hanging on even if barely. Samad got the ball he wanted, full and just outside off. Went for the step-hit, rotational arc with the bat face looking at the sky. Scraped it flat to the fielder
Pitched up, driven down the ground
Livingstone charges but can't stop it. Very low full toss, drilled back past the bowler. All along the ground. Pant brings up fifty, no celebration
Low full toss outside off, flicked aerially to long-on
Short and wide, sits up proper. Cut hard to deep cover, where Nitish fumbles and allows the second
Low full toss outside off, looks to go over cover, gets it to the sweeper along the ground

Appeal for caught behind. Bowler not keen. No review taken. Was a shortish pace-on ball outside off. Mukul went across, got inside the line and looked to scoop it while standing mostly upright. Misses
Another dot, cue ended back to the bowler. Was a shin-high full toss on off. Mukul pre-meditated again, got across and went for the sweep.

Given lbw, LSG review. Will be a dot either way. Was a highish full toss that hit the outer-half of the back thigh. Mukul swung across the line and missed... and got hit outside the line of the stumps, Hawk-Eye shows

It is yorker first ball... but is drilled to the straight boundary! Long-on chased to the right, no long-off. Beats the dive. Very good ball, at the stumps but Pant somehow got underneath and drove it through

Shot of the match and that's the game sealed! Pant's upper-body leant to the leg side, contorted himself again and flat-batted a shortish ball, from shoulder-height mind you, and flat-batted it over mid-off tennis style
Wide yorker is chomped to cover, fielder's well in
Driven... straight to mid-off. Mukul takes off and is sent back early. Low full toss, Pant hitting it along the ground
Third time's the charm and Pant wins it for LSG! Gentle tap of the glove to the partner. Wide, full ball, Pant gets on one knee and with a mostly horizontal bat, slaps it over mid-off
oh! Googly on a back of a length on off. Noor swings at it with abandon and misses. Lucky to see his stumps still intact
googly on a length, angling into the pads. Noor plays all around it and the ball rolls off the front pad into the on side
slower ball on a full length outside off. Kamboj gives it all he has and bashes it through the bowler's hands. Tough chance
back of a length outside off. Noor has a wild mow at it and chunks it off the inside edge towards short fine leg
Noor with a clean hit! In the arc on off. Seems like the knuckle ball and Noor picks it, swiping it handsomely over long on!
off-pace again, on a back of a length outside off. Noor throws the kitchen sink at it and slices it towards deep point
hard length just outside off. Kamboj jams it to long off
short and wide outside off. Noor swishes at it and misses. Called a wide too
too wide outside off. Kamboj slashes hard at it and misses. Gets the wide, though
zinged in very full on the pads. Kamboj is late on the flick and gets jammed on the back leg
into the blockhole on middle and off. Kamboj digs it out towards deep mid wicket
banged in short outside off. Henry gets up on his toes and flat-bats it over the bowler's head
Kamboj takes CSK past 200! Slower ball banged in short outside off. Kamboj winds up and hoicks it over deep square leg!
yorker outside off. Kamboj jams down on it and clinks it out into the leg side
full toss outside off. Kamboj is late on the stroke and jabs it into the off side
excellent yorker, zoning into leg stump. Henry flicks it down to long on
safe again! Hard length on off. Kamboj incredibly late on the pull and he splices it just beyond the bowler

taken at deep mid wicket, and RCB make it four wins in a row against CSK for the first time ever! And they have done it at an absolute canter! Slower ball into the track outside off. Henry has a swipe at it and drags it towards deep mid wicket, where Salt accepts the offering with glee!
Smart batting! Back of a length on middle and off. Shardul moves across his stumps early and scoops it over short fine!
Very full on middle and off. Shardul cannot get under this and drills it to long on
back of a length outside off. Dhir reaches out for it and scuffs it towards extra cover
over short third again! Back of a length and wide outside off. Dhir peels the outer cover off the ball and scythes it away!

catching practice for long on again! On a length on middle and off. The angle from around the wicket, though, cramps Dhir just a shade, much like Hardik earlier in the evening. Arrows it straight down Bishnoi's throat, and MI are now seven down!
more runs! Not off the bat, though. Angled in on a length on leg. Chahar fails to make contact and the ball brushes the thigh pad before dribbling away past the keeper!
length ball hung up wide outside off. Shardul has a wild swish at it and misses the ball by a fair distance
full toss speared down leg. Shardul misses it but this time, the umpire opts to indulge in some exercise
well done on the fence! Length ball outside off. Shardul winds up and launches it over the bowler's head. Jaiswal hustles to his right from long off and cuts it off
too wide again, and this is not great bowling, especially with the game more or less in the bag. More extras for MI
shortish outside off. Boult opens up his front hip and hacks it to long on
short, wide and put away! Ample width on offer and Chahar frees his arms, slicing it over backward point. Deep point cannot get to it either!

Boult has been run out! Sizzling yorker on middle and leg. Boult squeezes it out towards the bowler. Chahar calls him through for the single but there was very little chance Boult would make it. Archer fields it on his follow-through, takes aim and throws down the stumps at his end!
slower ball on a back of a length on middle and leg. Chahar has a mow across the line and only swats away a few flies in the process
very full on middle and off. Chahar cannot do much with it and powers it down the ground to long on
Bumrah with a boundary too! Banged in short and wide. Bumrah reaches out for it and uses the pace to glide it over backward point!
slower bumper outside off. Bumrah loads up and heaves it between long on and deep mid wicket. Back for a brace

on the bounce to Hetmyer, but it does not matter in the grand scheme. RR win and they win at a canter! Slower short ball outside off. Chahar swishes at it and slices it towards sweeper cover. RR go 3-0 and MI, after beginning their campaign with a win, have now been subjected to successive defeats!

got him!! Rahul falls for 92. This could be the game for GT. Rahul chases a wide delivery, and falls. A cross-seam delivery from Siraj, who bowls on a yorker length and wide outside off. Rahul had left the previous wide balls outside off this over, but not this time. He has a swing at it, and ends up getting an outside edge for Buttler
short ball on middle, and shapes away. Pulled to deep midwicket
lands between point and deep point running towards each other. A short-of-a-length ball outside off, and Nigam sliced it aerially
gets across the stumps, and looks to scoop, but misses. Rabada saw the batter moving, and bowled it short and wide of off. The ball went over Nigam in the end
this time Nigam backs away. Short ball outside off, and swatted to long-on
crashed over extra cover. Short delivery finishing on off, and he waits before tennis-batting the ball over the in-field
short again, and pulled from middle to fine leg. There's a cry from Miller, possibly on hitting the ball straight to the fielder
wide ball. Siraj angles it across, and goes on a yorker length wide of off. Left alone by Miller

six more?! I think it landed bang on the boundary cushion. But the third umpire will confirm. Siraj went full this time, but the line was wide of off. Miller launched the ball forcefully, and went over extra cover. We're still waiting for confirmation regarding it was a four or six. Oh, it's four.

Miller doesn't even need to look. He still gets six. His eyes are facing the ground, even as the ball has found its way into the sky. Full delivery around middle, and thumped back over long-on for six more. Who would believe Miller had hurt his hand while both fielding and batting?!
short ball again. This pitches outside leg, and Miller pulls after the ball finishes on middle. Single to deep midwicket

four for Nigam!! All Buttler can do is watch the ball sail over his head. Bouncer from Siraj, and this pitches outside off but ends on middle. There's a man at deep third, but the late upper cut is late enough for Nigam to get four
Nigam backs away, and Siraj follows. It ends up being a high-ish full toss way outside leg. Nigam whips to fine leg, even as I thought they wouldn't run
over mid-off. Why do you need a Miller when a Nigam is on song?! He's deep in the crease, and slices this length ball on the stumps. There's no long-off, and Nigam has all the room to swing his arms

taken by Gill!! More importantly for GT, Miller will remain off strike. More drama. A short ball from Prasidh, who bowls a cutter outside off. The ball shapes back in, and Nigam decides to swat it back after making room. But he can only send it up for Gill to take it after moving a few steps back from extra cover
a loud cheer from the crowd as Kuldeep takes the single. Short delivery just outside off, and Kuldeep guides it to deep third

this lands out of the ground!! A few hundred miles over long-off. Gill looks away. We'll need a new ball. Miller gets a length delivery outside off, and he liberally lofts it through the line. He got the pace and the length to go for it.
ohhh, he sends Kuldeep back. Dot ball for GT. Wow, Miller will need two off one ball. Short ball, and he pulled from middle to deep-backward square leg

Have GT have won by one run?! This is an amazing finish. The umpire has given Kuldeep run-out, but hang on... this could be wide for height. Plenty is happening even as GT look confident. The players are lurking on the field, waiting for the final decision. Ohh, the ball passes below Miller's head, although he's "on his toes", as per the third umpire. More wait. More nerves. More drama. If it's not a wide, GT will win by one run. Indeed. The ball passes well below the head height. So it's not a wide . So it's confirmed: GT win by one run . This was the delivery: Prasidh bowled a slower one, and short in length. Miller looked to pull but missed. The ball shaped away just before landing into Buttler's gloves. Miller and Kuldeep ran, but the latter fell short despite diving as Buttler hit the stumps at his end
